title: Pope returns to Rome on plane offered by Spanish king after technical glitch, Vatican says
content: It was the first time in decades that a papal flight had experienced a problem so serious that it required the pope to change planes.
snippet: It was the first time in decades that a papal flight had experienced a problem so serious that it required the pope to change planes.

title: EU countries agree to unblock accession talks with Ukraine and Moldova
content: Friday's agreement allows Ukraine and Moldova to formally open the first cluster of accession negotiations, known as "Fundamentals".
snippet: Friday's agreement allows Ukraine and Moldova to formally open the first cluster of accession negotiations, known as "Fundamentals".

title: 'Hussarz' roar over Poland as first F-35s officially enter service
content: Residents of several Polish cities heard the roar of F-35 fighter engines as the first three jets delivered to Poland staged a ceremonial flypast over Gdańsk, Warsaw, Kraków and Łódź.
snippet: Residents of several Polish cities heard the roar of F-35 fighter engines as the first three jets delivered to Poland staged a ceremonial flypast over Gdańsk, Warsaw, Kraków and Łódź.

title: Belgian driver convicted of murder over carnival car crash that killed seven people
content: The trial was heard in a special hall to accommodate nearly 200 civil parties taking part. The civil claimants include some who are disabled for life and the relatives of those who died.
snippet: The trial was heard in a special hall to accommodate nearly 200 civil parties taking part. The civil claimants include some who are disabled for life and the relatives of those who died.

title: Any change to EU protection scheme for Ukrainians should be 'gradual', UNHCR deputy chief says
content: Kelly Clements tells Euronews that any planned changes to EU protections for Ukrainian refugees should be carried out "very gradually", as some EU capitals push for excluding military-age men from the scheme.

title: The path to SAFE II: inside the struggle to revamp Europe's defence financing tool
content: EU policymakers are weighing a SAFE II defence financing tool, with Eastern-flank states pushing for grants over loans. But the final SAFE I uptake, the development of the European defence industry, Ukraine's battlefield-driven tech edge and shifting warfare scenarios are shaping what comes next.

title: US withdraws long-range military capabilities from NATO
content: The move comes at a particularly tense moment, with US Donald Trump increasingly angry at NATO allies over their refusal to join his war on Iran.
snippet: The move comes at a particularly tense moment, with US Donald Trump increasingly angry at NATO allies over their refusal to join his war on Iran.

title: EU energy ministers eye jet fuel reserves as Strait of Hormuz crisis threatens supply

title: Washington plans to slash fighter jets and warships to NATO in Europe, US media report
content: The reported cutbacks come as European nations race to bolster their defence capacities since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 sparked fears that Moscow could attack a NATO country.
snippet: The reported cutbacks come as European nations race to bolster their defence capacities since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 sparked fears that Moscow could attack a NATO country.

title: Pope urges 'reciprocal' integration between migrants and host countries

title: ‘Land of the fulfilled farmers’: Why the grass seems to grow greener in Austria
content: Austria has repeatedly been acknowledged as a success story in the intergenerational renewal of its farming sector. A combination of hard work at family-run farms, strong public support, qualitative professional training and engaged consumers is revitalizing entire rural communities.

title: How EU countries watered down 'solidarity' plans to relocate asylum seekers
content: A new migration law introducing "mandatory solidarity" has been all but circumvented by the EU's member states, who will relocate fewer than 9,000 asylum seekers this year – even after the annual minimum was set at 30,000.

title: Everything you need to know about the Jared Kushner resort protests in Albania
content: The European Commission has warned Albania that it must act now to avoid jeopardising its bid to join the bloc, which requires it to comply with EU environmental rules.
snippet: The European Commission has warned Albania that it must act now to avoid jeopardising its bid to join the bloc, which requires it to comply with EU environmental rules.

title: Georgieva: Europeans must invest 'together'
content: European economic conditions "have worsened," International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said, as the IMF is now "downgrading" its growth projections and "uplifting" its inflation forecasts. Georgieva recommends that the EU invest "together" and "wisely".

title: Three children dead after car ploughs into school cycling group in the Netherlands

title: Senior MEP fears Airbus-Boeing dispute could reignite EU-US tensions

title: Cyprus proposes €32.8bn cut to next EU budget as compromise between rival camps
content: Cyprus proposes a 2% cut — worth €32.8bn — to the EU's draft €2 trillion budget for 2028–2034, in a bid to bridge the gap between "frugals" and cohesion-minded member states. The figures, now on the table for the first time, will serve as the starting point for the sensitive negotiations ahead.

title: Why Europe's labour needs clash with its migration policy
content: Europe faces a paradox: economies need workers while governments tighten migration rules. This tension grows as the EU’s Migration and Asylum Pact takes effect on 12 June, marking the bloc’s strictest migration policy in decades.

title: World Cup: Has Trump already ruined the vibes? MEPs debate in The Ring
content: The World Cup is on, the single biggest sport event of the year, followed by millions of people around the world. So far, the vibes are strangely subdued. Ticket sales and hotel bookings are below expectations and immigration issues are making headlines - especially in the US.
